journey.name: SSIM Schedule Import Flow
journey.description: End-to-end journey covering SSIM file import via the Operations API. The Operations API parses the SSIM file, converts each Type 3 scheduled-passenger leg to the season schedule JSON format, and forwards the full payload to the Schedule MS POST /v1/schedules, which atomically replaces all existing schedule records. Tests successful import, validation error handling, and empty-body rejection.

steps.name: Import SSIM — empty body
steps.description: Submit a SSIM import request with an empty body. Validates that the Operations API rejects the request before attempting to parse or forward anything.

body.error: Request body must contain SSIM file content.

expected.description: 400 Bad Request — request body is empty. The Operations API validates that the body is non-empty before attempting to parse. No call is made to Schedule MS.
expected.validationNotes: An empty or whitespace-only body is rejected immediately at the Operations API boundary.

steps.name: Import SSIM — no valid Type 3 records
steps.description: Submit a SSIM file that contains only header and trailer records (no Type 3 flight leg records). Validates that the Operations API returns 400 when the parsed record set is empty.

body.error: No valid Type 3 scheduled-passenger records found in the SSIM file.

expected.description: 400 Bad Request — SSIM file contains no Type 3 records. The Operations API SsimParser finds zero flight leg records after processing and returns an error.
expected.validationNotes: The SSIM file must contain at least one Type 3 record with service type Y (scheduled passenger). Files with only Type 1, 2, or 5 records are rejected.

steps.name: Import SSIM — 2026 season schedule (48 flights)
steps.description: Import the full 2026 season SSIM file for Apex Air (48 scheduled-passenger flight legs, hub LHR, routes to JFK/LAX/MIA/SFO/ORD/BOS/BGI/KIN/NAS/HKG/NRT/PVG/PEK/SIN/BOM/DEL/BLR). The Operations API parses the SSIM file, builds the season schedule JSON payload, and forwards to Schedule MS which atomically replaces all existing schedule records.

expected.description: 200 OK — Operations API parses 48 Type 3 records from the SSIM file, builds the season schedule payload, and forwards to Schedule MS. Schedule MS deletes any pre-existing records and inserts 48 new FlightSchedule rows. Response contains imported=48, deleted (number of pre-existing records removed), and an array of 48 schedule summaries each with scheduleId, flightNumber, origin, destination, validFrom, validTo, and operatingDateCount.
expected.validationNotes: imported must equal 48. deleted indicates how many records were removed (0 if table was empty beforehand). schedules array must contain exactly 48 entries. Each entry must have a non-empty UUID scheduleId, valid IATA flightNumber (e.g. AX001), 3-char origin and destination IATA codes, ISO 8601 validFrom and validTo dates, and a positive operatingDateCount. Daily flights (daysOfWeek=127) over a full year should show operatingDateCount=365.

steps.name: Re-import SSIM — confirms full replacement
steps.description: Re-import the same 2026 season SSIM file. Validates that the Schedule MS atomically replaces all records: deleted count should equal the number previously imported (48), and imported count should again equal 48.

expected.description: 200 OK — second import confirms full-replacement semantics. deleted must equal 48 (the records inserted in Step 3 are removed before the new set is inserted). imported must again equal 48. All scheduleId values in the response will be new UUIDs, distinct from those returned in Step 3.
expected.validationNotes: deleted must equal 48. imported must equal 48. scheduleId values must differ from Step 3 (new UUIDs are generated on each import). This confirms the Schedule MS performs a full delete-and-reinsert rather than an upsert.
